# Section 329 Fee Disgorgement - Get Fees Back [2026]
> Last updated: 2026-05-01

> Section 329 lets courts order fee disgorgement from bankruptcy attorneys. Learn how to file, what evidence you need, and fee limits. 2026 guide.

## About
section329.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- 329(b) Motion for Fee Disgorgement - How to File: https://section329.org/329b-motion.html
- Bankruptcy Attorney Fee Rights - Section 330 Reasonable Compensation: https://section329.org/attorney-rights.html
- Attorney Fee Disclosure Requirements - Section 329(a): https://section329.org/disclosure-requirements.html
- Excessive Bankruptcy Attorney Fees - What Courts Consider: https://section329.org/excessive-fees.html
- Bankruptcy Attorney Fee Disclosure - Section 329(a): https://section329.org/fee-disclosure.html
- How to File a Section 329 Fee Disgorgement Motion: https://section329.org/how-to-file.html
- Section 329 Fee Disgorgement - Get Fees Back: https://section329.org/
- UST Review of Bankruptcy Attorney Fees: https://section329.org/ust-fee-review.html
- Privacy Policy: https://section329.org/privacy.html

- Website: https://openbankruptcyproject.org

Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
